metadata.title: Why CLAUDE.md Breaks When It Gets Too Long
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Claude Code for Teachers candidate 06. The mechanism: CLAUDE.md's instructions compete with other context for Claude's attention; beyond ~200 lines the probabilistic instruction-following degrades and key rules are ignored; the fix is to keep CLAUDE.md lean by offloading workflow content to Skills and enforcing inviolable rules as Hooks.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = short CLAUDE.md / rule followed; CRIMSON = long CLAUDE.md / rule ignored; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: NO context-window math or token-count discussion, NO comparison with other tools' context files, NO full Skills/Hooks explanation. One mechanism: CLAUDE.md length degrades compliance; stay under 200 lines, offload to Skills/Hooks.

beats.narration_text: The teacher's CLAUDE.md has a clear rule on line twelve: Vanilla CSS only — no Tailwind. The project is on its third build. Claude generates a component. It uses Tailwind classes. The rule is still there on line twelve.

card.copy: Why CLAUDE.md breaks when it gets too long

beats.narration_text: Session one: forty-seven lines. Stack rules. Code style. Environment quirks. Never-rules. Claude follows the Vanilla-CSS rule every time. Session two: eighty lines. Grading workflow notes. New never-rules. Lessons-learned entries. Still working. Session three: the simulation build. Sprint goals added. Architecture notes. A colleague's phone number. Four near-duplicate CSS rules. Two-hundred-twenty lines.

production_viz.label: CLAUDE.md grows: 47 → 80 → 220 lines across three builds
production_viz.mechanic: a document bar growing taller in three steps — 47 lines (teal), 80 lines (teal), 220 lines (crimson at the top portion); a compliance meter beside it starts high and begins to dip at 220

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] growing document bar with compliance meter

beats.narration_text: Here is the question. Adding more rules to CLAUDE.md should make Claude more reliable. Here is the project where Claude began ignoring key rules as CLAUDE.md grew past two hundred lines. Why?

CLAUDE.md is advisory. Claude reads it at session start. It tries to follow it. The compliance is probabilistic — Claude may sometimes generate code that violates a CLAUDE.md rule, especially under prompt pressure. In a short file, the Vanilla CSS rule is prominent. In a three-hundred-line file, it competes with sprint goals, duplicate entries, personal reminders, and workflow notes that should be in a Skill.

production_viz.label: the rule competes with noise — attention diluted
production_viz.mechanic: a document with the 'Vanilla CSS only' rule visible at the top; as the document grows, new crimson entries (sprint goals, duplicates, personal notes) pile in; the rule visually recedes as the noise accumulates

beats.narration_text: The example. After the simulation build, the teacher's CLAUDE.md is at two-hundred-twenty lines. Claude generates Tailwind classes on a new component despite Vanilla CSS only on line twelve. The teacher trims to ninety-five lines. Moves grading workflow to a Skill. Converts the grade-generation rule to a Hook. Claude respects the CSS rule again.
beats.scene_description: a printed document page with line twelve highlighted and 'Vanilla CSS only' visible, many other lines visible below and above, editorial collage, desaturated
beats.image_prompt: B05, printed CLAUDE.md document page with line twelve highlighted in faint teal — the text 'Vanilla CSS only' readable — surrounded by many other lines of text above and below, some text blurred, document pinned to aged newsprint, editorial collage style, highly desaturated, cream and charcoal tones
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL ai] CLAUDE.md with rule on line 12 amid long document

beats.narration_text: The fix has three moves. Keep CLAUDE.md under two hundred lines — the five categories that belong in it: bash commands, code style deviations, test runners, architectural decisions, environment quirks. Move workflow content to Skills — on-demand files that are not loaded at session start. Convert inviolable rules to Hooks — scripts that execute regardless of what Claude decides.

production_viz.label: three moves: trim to 200, move workflows to Skills, convert rules to Hooks
production_viz.mechanic: three rows drawing in teal: 'CLAUDE.md: under 200 lines — five categories only'; 'Skills: workflow content — loaded on demand'; 'Hooks: inviolable rules — execute regardless'

beats.narration_text: The compliance meter. Short CLAUDE.md: rule-following high. Past two hundred lines: compliance begins to degrade. Past three hundred: key rules get ignored under prompt pressure. The meter is not about line count for its own sake — it is about signal-to-noise. Every entry added dilutes every other entry.

production_viz.label: compliance meter: high at 50 lines, dips at 200, fails at 300
production_viz.mechanic: a horizontal axis showing line count (50, 100, 200, 300); a compliance meter curve starting high (teal), staying flat to 150, beginning to dip at 200 (amber), falling at 300 (crimson); labeled milestones

beats.narration_text: The practical move: audit your CLAUDE.md now. For each entry: does Claude know this from the code already? Cut. Does this change session to session? Move it to TODO.md. Is it a workflow? Move it to a Skill. Must it hold every time without exception? Convert it to a Hook. What remains is what belongs.

production_viz.label: four audit questions for each CLAUDE.md entry
production_viz.mechanic: a four-row decision table drawing in: 'Claude infers it from code -> cut'; 'changes session to session -> move to TODO.md'; 'a workflow -> move to Skill'; 'must hold always -> convert to Hook'

beats.narration_text: A maintained fifty-line CLAUDE.md beats an abandoned five-hundred-line one every time. The rule on line twelve works when it is the most specific thing on the page. It stops working when it is noise.

card.copy: Short and specific. Not long and aspirational.
